title: Winter Home Safety for Aging Parents
summary: Winter brings its own specific set of home safety concerns worth revisiting each year, even in an already safety-checked home.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Winter Home Safety for Aging Parents | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Winter brings its own specific set of home safety concerns worth revisiting each year, even in an already safety-checked home.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Icy walkways are a major fall-risk factor unique to the season.
Heating equipment safety deserves an annual check.
Power outages carry higher stakes for anyone using medical equipment or refrigerated medication.

paragraphs: Icy or snow-covered walkways and steps present a seasonal fall risk that a general home safety check earlier in the year wouldn't have caught — arranging for reliable snow and ice removal before winter arrives is worth prioritizing.

Heating equipment, particularly space heaters and older furnaces, deserves an annual safety check, and for anyone using medical equipment or refrigerated medications, a power outage plan matters more in winter than perhaps any other season.

nextSteps: Arrange reliable snow and ice removal for walkways before winter arrives.
Build a specific power outage plan for anyone using medical equipment or refrigerated medication.
disclaimer: General seasonal guidance — building-specific and regional risks may call for additional precautions.
